sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Commercial Metals Comp stock hits all-time high at 84.34 USD
feed.summary: Commercial Metals Company (CMC) stock has reached an all-time high of $84.34, marking a 69.6% appreciation over the past year. This growth is attributed to strong market position, favorable market conditions, strategic initiatives, and robust demand in the metals industry. Despite potentially trading above its Fair Value, CMC maintains a "GOOD" financial health rating and has consistently paid dividends for 56 years, further boosted by recent strong financial results and a raised price target from Jefferies.

feed.title: Balchem Corporation stock hits 52-week high at 177.41 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Balchem Corporation's stock has reached a new 52-week high of $177.41 USD, reflecting an 11.32% growth over the past year. The company has a strong record of financial stability, maintaining dividend payments for 40 consecutive years and raising them for 16 straight years. Recent developments include a 10.3% increase in its annual dividend and a "Neutral" rating with a $162.00 price target from Rothschild Redburn, highlighting its resilient business model.

feed.title: Old Second Bancorp: Regional Bank Stock Tries To Hold Its Ground As Rates, Results And Recession Fea
feed.summary: Old Second Bancorp's stock has seen a mild decline recently, underperforming larger banks but aligning with regional peers, as investors cautiously assess the regional banking sector. Despite solid profitability and credit quality, the stock is trading below its 52-week highs, reflecting investor concerns about future interest rates, funding costs, and potential credit quality weakening. Analysts maintain a generally constructive outlook, rating OSBC as Buy to Overweight, but acknowledge that its future performance hinges on interest rate trajectories, sustained credit quality, and organic growth in a competitive Midwest market.

feed.title: Top Research Reports for Alphabet, Caterpillar & T-Mobile
feed.summary: This article highlights top research reports from Zacks Equity Research for February 12, 2026, featuring analyses of major stocks like Alphabet (GOOGL), Caterpillar (CAT), and T-Mobile (TMUS), along with microcap Onfolio Holdings (ONFO). It details the financial performance and future prospects of each company, noting strengths such as Alphabet's AI growth and Google Cloud backlog, Caterpillar's record backlog and segment growth, and T-Mobile's postpaid customer additions, while also acknowledging challenges like competition and market saturation.

feed.title: Blog Update: $623,738 Sanctions Ordered in Golikov v. Walmart Inc.
feed.summary: A blog update reports that in December, Judge Klausner ordered two plaintiff's lawyers to pay $623,738.70 in sanctions in the case of Golikov v. Walmart Inc. The sanctions were imposed because the lawyers unreasonably and vexatiously multiplied proceedings, alleging a purchase from a store instead of the defendant's website, which would have required a binding arbitration agreement. The awarded sanctions covered 842.8 hours of work by 14 defense lawyers at rates ranging from $540 to $1,237.50/hour.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. (TMO): Our Calculation of Intrinsic Value
feed.summary: This article presents a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis for Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. (TMO), calculating an intrinsic value per share of approximately $187. Despite recognizing TMO as a high-quality company with a strong competitive position and steady free cash flow, the analysis concludes that its current market price of ~$527 suggests a substantial overvaluation with a -65% margin of safety. The authors advise that future returns will likely depend more on business execution and end-market growth than on valuation expansion at the current price.

feed.title: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings names John Chidsey as new CEO By Investing.com
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. has appointed John W. Chidsey as its new President and CEO, effective immediately. Chidsey, who has prior experience as CEO of Subway Restaurants and Burger King Holdings, Inc., replaces Harry Sommer. The company expects its Q4 2025 net yield to be around the midpoint of its previously disclosed range and core quarterly and full-year 2025 results to align with guidance.

feed.title: Harry Sommer Out at Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings, Board Director Named as Replacement
feed.summary: Harry Sommer has stepped down as President, CEO, and director of Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH), with immediate effect. John W. Chidsey, a current director of NCLH and former CEO of Subway Restaurants, has been appointed as his replacement. Chidsey's immediate priorities include sharpening execution, improving financial performance, reducing leverage, and enhancing long-term shareholder value.

feed.title: Dexcom Q4 2025 presentation slides: double-digit growth amid margin expansion
feed.summary: Dexcom (NASDAQ:DXCM) reported strong Q4 2025 results, with 12% organic revenue growth and significant margin expansion, despite a post-earnings stock decline. The company's international segment showed stronger growth than the U.S. market, and strategic initiatives like the G7 15 Day CGM and FDA clearance for Smart Basal are set to drive future expansion. Dexcom provided optimistic 2026 guidance, projecting continued revenue growth and margin improvement while acknowledging potential market risks.

feed.title: GE HealthCare will pay 3.5¢ per share to investors in May
feed.summary: GE HealthCare Technologies Inc. announced a cash dividend of $0.035 per share for the first quarter of 2026. The dividend is payable on May 15, 2026, to shareholders of record as of April 3, 2026. The company is a global healthcare solutions provider focused on medical technology and pharmaceutical diagnostics.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Federal Realty beats Q4 2025 expectations with strong EPS
feed.summary: Federal Realty Investment Trust (FRT) reported strong financial results for Q4 2025, with EPS of $1.48 (double the forecast) and revenue of $336.05 million, exceeding expectations. Despite these positive results, the stock saw a 2.06% decline in after-hours trading. The company also provided optimistic FFO guidance for 2026 and highlighted strategic growth initiatives including residential development and asset recycling.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Electrovaya Q1 2026 shows strong growth, stock dips
feed.summary: Electrovaya Inc. reported strong Q1 2026 results with a 39% year-over-year revenue increase to $15.5 million and a net profit of $1 million. Despite this growth, the stock experienced a dip, reflecting investor concerns regarding future production timelines and overall market conditions. The company reaffirmed its 30% revenue growth target for fiscal 2026 and highlighted expansion plans, including the Jamestown facility and diversification into defense and robotics sectors.

feed.title: Harrah’s Oklahoma to open April 9 as first Caesars-managed casino in the state
feed.summary: Harrah's Oklahoma, a new casino developed in partnership with the Iowa Tribe of Oklahoma, is set to open on April 9, 2026, becoming the first Caesars Entertainment-managed casino in the state. The property will offer a Las Vegas-style gaming, dining, and entertainment experience, including table games, slot machines, and multiple restaurants. This venture is expected to create over 300 new jobs and drive economic growth and community investment for the Iowa Tribe.

feed.title: 5 Must-Read Analyst Questions From American Express's Q4 Earnings Call
feed.summary: American Express's Q4 earnings fell short of expectations, attributed by management to strategic investments in premium products and ongoing global product refreshes. Despite the financial miss, CEO Stephen Squeri highlighted strong customer demand, engagement, and credit quality. The article focuses on key analyst questions from the earnings call, covering topics like the impact of the shift to premium products, competitive landscape in commercial services, and the effect of product refreshes on customer engagement.

feed.title: Sysco Earns Top Honors from National Cattlemen’s Beef Association (NCBA)
feed.summary: Sysco, the world's largest foodservice company, has been awarded the 2026 Beef Quality Assurance (BQA) Marketer of the Year by the National Cattlemen’s Beef Association (NCBA). This recognition honors Sysco's "Raised With Respect TM" initiative, a collaboration with Certified Angus Beef, which promotes responsible beef production practices and provides education and BQA certifications to farmers and ranchers. The program aims to build consumer trust by highlighting animal care and environmental stewardship in the beef industry.

feed.title: Prologis (PLD) Increases Dividend by 6%
feed.summary: Prologis (PLD) has announced a 6% increase in its annual dividend, bringing the payout to $4.28 per share, demonstrating its commitment to shareholder returns. The company, a leading global REIT focused on industrial and logistics facilities, shows strong financial performance with robust revenue growth and operating margins. While exhibiting premium valuation and positive analyst sentiment, investors should be mindful of sector-specific risks and the stock's higher volatility.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Dexcom Q4 2025 beats forecasts, stock dips
feed.summary: Dexcom (DXCM) reported Q4 2025 earnings exceeding forecasts with an EPS of $0.68 and revenue of $1.26 billion, yet its stock dipped 4.45% in after-hours trading. The company highlighted strong international growth, the launch of the Dexcom G7 15-day system, and anticipates Medicare coverage expansion for new patient segments. Dexcom also provided a robust 2026 revenue guidance between $5.16 billion and $5.25 billion, expecting continued growth driven by product innovation and market expansion.

feed.title: Looking To Compete With BioMarin, Ascendis Continues Its Growth Story
feed.summary: Ascendis Pharma A/S experienced significant sales growth in 2025 and anticipates a new product approval in 2026. This growth positions Ascendis to compete more directly with BioMarin Pharmaceutical Inc. The article suggests that Ascendis is continuing its positive trajectory in the market.

feed.title: Assessing Vulcan Materials (VMC) Valuation After Recent Share Price Weakness And Strong Longer Term Gains
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials (VMC) has seen a recent share price decline despite strong longer-term gains, prompting an assessment of its valuation. While a fair value estimate suggests the stock is slightly undervalued at $319.78, its high P/E ratio of 37.6x raises questions about the margin of safety for investors. The article encourages investors to conduct their own analysis, considering both the positive narrative of infrastructure growth and potential risks like project delays or funding disruptions.

feed.title: Dynagas LNG Partners Declares Q4 2025 Cash Distribution of $0.05 per Common Unit
feed.summary: Dynagas LNG Partners (DLNG) has announced a cash distribution of $0.05 per common unit for the fourth quarter of 2025, payable on February 27, 2026, to unitholders of record as of February 23, 2026. This distribution highlights the company's consistent cash generation from its fleet of six LNG carriers operating under multi-year charters. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates DLNG as "Outperform" due to its strong financial performance, high profitability, robust cash generation, and attractive valuation, despite some tempered by weakening revenue and mixed near-term technical momentum.

feed.title: Federal Realty (FRT) Exceeds Q4 Revenue Expectations with Robust Growth
feed.summary: Federal Realty (FRT) surpassed Q4 revenue expectations, reporting $336.04 million against an anticipated $329.02 million, driven by strong leasing and rent increases. The company is actively pursuing strategic objectives including capital recycling, reinvestment, and expansion into new markets, despite refinancing challenges. Federal Realty forecasts a 6% increase in Core FFO by 2026, supported by its strategic initiatives and market position in high-growth metropolitan areas.

feed.title: Cardinal Health Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.5107 per Share, Payable April 15 to Holders of Record April 1
feed.summary: Cardinal Health announced its quarterly dividend will remain at $0.5107 per share. The dividend is payable on April 15, 2026, to shareholders of record as of April 1, 2026. This news comes amidst various analyst target price adjustments and a maintained 'Buy' or 'Outperform' rating from several firms.

feed.title: Applied Materials jumps on earnings strength and bullish forecast tied to AI chip boom
feed.summary: Applied Materials Inc. stock soared over 12% in after-hours trading following a strong fiscal first-quarter report that surpassed analyst expectations for both earnings and revenue. The semiconductor equipment maker's positive outlook for the coming quarter, driven by the increasing demand for AI chips, also contributed to the bullish sentiment. CEO Gary Dickerson anticipates over 20% growth in their semiconductor equipment business this calendar year, focusing on high-performance and energy-efficient chips.

feed.title: Prologis Board of Directors Approves 6 Percent Increase in Quarterly Common Stock Dividend
feed.summary: Prologis, Inc.'s Board of Directors has approved a 6% increase in the company's annualized dividend, raising it to $4.28 per share of common stock. The board declared a quarterly cash dividend of $1.07 per common share and $1.0675 per share of 8.54% Series Q Cumulative Redeemable Preferred Stock, both payable on March 31, 2026. These dividends are for stockholders of record as of March 17, 2026.

feed.title: IBM Faces AI Threat, Not Opportunity, Says Short Seller Who Predicted Enron’s Collapse
feed.summary: Jim Chanos, the short seller famous for predicting Enron's downfall, argues that AI poses a threat rather than an opportunity for IBM, which he describes as primarily a marketing and consulting company, not a cutting-edge AI leader. He highlighted IBM's "India-centric body shop model" and recent software acquisitions, noting that its true innovations in the 21st century have been in financial engineering. Despite IBM's recent strong Q4 2025 earnings, which saw a 12% revenue growth and beat Wall Street estimates, Chanos maintains his skeptical outlook on the company's AI narrative.

feed.title: L3Harris Technologies (LHX) Files for Mixed Securities Shelf Off
feed.summary: L3Harris Technologies (LHX) has filed an automatic mixed securities shelf registration, giving it flexibility to raise capital. While the company shows strong revenue growth and a good market position in the Aerospace & Defense sector, investors should note its declining margins and high valuation metrics. This strategic financial move allows L3Harris to offer various types of securities for future initiatives, amidst concerns about its profitability and market sentiment.

feed.title: Hormel Foods (HRL) Appoints New Head of Supply Chain Operations
feed.summary: Hormel Foods has appointed Will Bonifant as Group Vice President and Chief Supply Chain Officer, effective March 9. Bonifant, previously from Hershey, will oversee Hormel's global supply chain operations, including procurement, manufacturing, logistics, and innovation. This strategic hire aims to enhance the company's supply chain efficiency and innovation as Hormel continues to evolve its product offerings in the consumer packaged goods sector.

feed.title: Cineverse (CNVS) Announces Public Offering of Class A Shares
feed.summary: Cineverse (CNVS) has announced an underwritten public offering of its Class A common stock, with The Benchmark Company as the sole underwriter, to raise capital and expand its market presence. The company, which operates in streaming technology and entertainment, faces financial challenges including a negative net margin and a low Altman Z-Score indicating financial distress. Investors are advised to be cautious despite the potential capital injection, given the company's current financial health and market volatility.

feed.title: Jeanine Pirro sues Con-Ed , NY town over trip at construction site that caused her brain bruising
feed.summary: Jeanine Pirro is suing Con Edison and the town of Rye, New York, claiming a "dangerous" construction site caused her to trip and suffer a brain contusion and other injuries last August. The lawsuit alleges negligence on the part of the company and the town, where Pirro resides. She is seeking damages for pain, suffering, inconvenience, and other losses, but the specific amount is not disclosed.

feed.title: Jeanine Pirro files $250K injury lawsuit against City of Rye, Con Edison
feed.summary: Jeanine Pirro has filed a $250,000 injury lawsuit against the City of Rye and Con Edison. She claims that a preventable injury left her bedridden. The lawsuit seeks damages for the injuries sustained.

feed.title: NASCAR® and Coca-Cola North America Extend Longstanding Partnership; BODYARMOR Named Official Sports Drink of NASCAR
feed.summary: NASCAR and Coca-Cola North America have extended their long-standing Premier Partnership, with BODYARMOR Sports Drink now named the Official Sports Drink of NASCAR. This multi-year agreement expands Coca-Cola's involvement in the sport, integrating BODYARMOR into NASCAR's hydration category while maintaining Coca-Cola's designation as the Official Soft Drink. The partnership will feature robust activation platforms, including at-track experiences and expanded storytelling, and continue community impact initiatives.

feed.title: Nuburu Adjusts Orbit Deal Terms With Stock Consideration
feed.summary: Nuburu (BURU) has made several strategic moves, including acquiring a 0.8% stake in Heckler & Koch AG for $15 million via a subordinated convertible note to strengthen its defense and security platform. The company also retired Series A preferred shares by issuing a warrant and amended its Orbit S.r.l. acquisition terms to pay the remaining consideration with common stock. These actions aim to reduce liabilities, comply with NYSE equity rules, and finalize a related-party acquisition, though TipRanks' AI Analyst Spark rates BURU as Underperform due to financial instability.

feed.title: Ameriprise Financial (NYSE:AMP) CEO William Truscott Sells 5,000 Shares
feed.summary: Ameriprise Financial CEO William Truscott sold 5,000 shares of company stock for over $2.66 million, reducing his stake by 29.16%. The sale followed strong quarterly results where the company beat analyst expectations for EPS and revenue. Analysts currently have a mixed view on AMP, with a consensus target price of $555.67.

feed.title: PTC (NASDAQ:PTC) EVP Sells $38,664.00 in Stock
feed.summary: PTC's EVP Staats Aaron Von sold 240 shares of PTC stock for $38,664.00 on February 11th, reducing his ownership by 1.07% to 22,221 shares. The company recently exceeded quarterly earnings estimates with an EPS of $1.92 and revenue of $685.8 million, also providing optimistic Q2 2026 guidance. Despite a recent stock performance dip, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average price target of $188.08 for PTC.

feed.title: Public Storage (NYSE:PSA) Posts Earnings Results, Misses Estimates By $1.63 EPS
feed.summary: Public Storage (NYSE:PSA) reported earnings of $2.60 per share for the quarter, missing the consensus estimate of $4.23 by $1.63. Despite missing EPS estimates, the company's revenue of $1.22 billion slightly exceeded analyst expectations of $1.21 billion. The stock traded up 0.3% to $294.67 on Thursday with higher-than-average volume, and Wall Street analysts currently have a "Hold" rating with a consensus target price of $306.63.

feed.title: Benjamin Jackson Sells 12,861 Shares of Intercontinental Exchange (NYSE:ICE) Stock
feed.summary: Benjamin Jackson, President of Intercontinental Exchange (NYSE:ICE), sold 12,861 shares of company stock for over $2.12 million on February 10th, reducing his ownership by 7.20%. Despite this insider selling, ICE recently exceeded earnings expectations, reporting an EPS of $1.71 versus an estimated $1.67 and revenue of $3.14 billion. The company also announced a quarterly dividend of $0.52 per share and maintains a consensus "Buy" rating from Wall Street analysts with an average target price of $195.40.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Emerson Electric (NYSE:EMR) Director Sells 1,476 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: Emerson Electric's Director Matthew Levatich sold 1,476 shares of EMR stock for $239,348 on February 10th, decreasing his stake by 5.72%. This comes as EMR shares dropped 6.2% intraday and the company announced a quarterly dividend of $0.555, alongside beating EPS estimates and issuing FY2026 guidance. Institutional ownership remains high, with analysts holding a consensus "Hold" rating for the stock.

feed.title: Mark Begor Sells 37,791 Shares of Equifax (NYSE:EFX) Stock
feed.summary: Equifax CEO Mark Begor sold 37,791 shares of the company's stock for approximately $7.63 million, reducing his holding by 14.09%. This insider sale comes as Equifax shares dropped by 4.7% to $188.30, trading below their 50-day and 200-day moving averages, despite the company beating Q4 earnings estimates and setting optimistic FY2026 guidance. Analysts currently maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with a target price of $240.33, though some have recently lowered their price objectives.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Equifax (NYSE:EFX) EVP Sells 1,167 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: Equifax (NYSE:EFX) Executive Vice President Jamil Farshchi sold 1,167 shares of the company's stock on February 11th, totaling approximately $233,248. This sale reduced his holdings by 2.84% to 39,869 shares. The transaction follows a previous sale of 6,213 shares on February 10th and comes as Equifax exceeded Q4 earnings estimates, set favorable FY2026 EPS guidance, and maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts.

feed.title: Keith Sullivan Sells 40,976 Shares of Neuronetics (NASDAQ:STIM) Stock
feed.summary: Keith Sullivan, CEO of Neuronetics (NASDAQ:STIM), sold 40,976 shares of the company's stock on February 10th at an average price of $1.55, totaling $63,512.80. This transaction reduced his stake by 2.55% to 1,567,012 shares. Neuronetics currently has a consensus "Hold" rating from analysts with a target price of $7.00, and institutional investors own approximately 53.59% of the shares.

feed.title: Quest Diagnostics (NYSE:DGX) SVP Sells 5,662 Shares
feed.summary: Quest Diagnostics (NYSE:DGX) SVP Patrick Plewman sold 5,662 shares of the company's stock for over $1.15 million on February 10th, reducing his ownership by 26.91%. This comes after Quest Diagnostics reported better-than-expected Q4 earnings and revenue, raised its dividend, and authorized a $1 billion share buyback program. Analysts have largely maintained "Buy" or "Hold" ratings with an average target price of $208.93.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean Group prices $1.25B senior notes due 2033 and $1.25B senior notes due 2038
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group has priced two offerings of senior notes: $1.25 billion due in 2033 and another $1.25 billion due in 2038. This financial move aims to raise capital for the cruise line operator.

feed.title: cbdMD, Inc. to Host Conference Call to Discuss December 31, 2025 First Quarter Results – Company Announcement
feed.summary: cbdMD, Inc. will host a conference call on Tuesday, February 17, 2026, at 4:20 p.m. Eastern Time, to discuss its first quarter financial results for December 31, 2025, and business developments. The company, known for its CBD and Delta 9 products, as well as its Paw CBD and ATRx brands, also operates Herbal Oasis, a THC-infused social seltzer. Interested parties can access the call via phone or webcast.

feed.title: Investors get Feb. 17 peek at cbdMD first-quarter results, 4:20 p.m. ET
feed.summary: cbdMD, Inc. (NYSE American: YCBD) will host a conference call on Tuesday, February 17, 2026, at 4:20 p.m. ET to discuss its first-quarter financial results for the period ended December 31, 2025. Investors can join the call via a dial-in number for USA/Canada or through a webcast, which will also be available for replay until February 17, 2027. The company will discuss financial performance and business progress.

feed.title: Blackstone Leads the Race to Unlock $7 Trillion of Cash in Japan
feed.summary: Blackstone Inc. is leading a campaign to attract Japanese private wealth by promoting alternative asset investments. With a significant media presence, including a television commercial featuring co-founder Steve Schwarzman, Blackstone aims to tap into the $7 trillion in cash held by Japanese households. This effort, alongside rivals like EQT AB and KKR & Co., targets the largest private wealth opportunity outside the US.

feed.title: Snap-on Incorporated Declares Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Snap-on Incorporated announced that its board of directors declared a quarterly common stock dividend of $2.44 per share. This dividend is payable on March 10, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 24, 2026. The company has a long history of paying consecutive quarterly cash dividends since 1939.

feed.title: Valley National Bancorp’s Preferred Shares Face a Pivotal Rate Shift
feed.summary: Investors in Valley National Bancorp's Series B preferred stock are at a critical point as the security's variable dividend, tied to interest rates since late 2022, will be affected by anticipated rate cuts. The regional bank sector remains complex, with emphasis on revenue diversification and careful observation of smaller banks. The upcoming earnings report on April 23, 2026, will be crucial for assessing the company's competitive standing amidst monetary policy shifts.

feed.title: Alexandria Equities Prices $750 Million Senior Notes Offering
feed.summary: Alexandria Real Estate Equities, Inc. (ARE) has successfully priced a $750 million offering of 5.25% senior notes due in 2036. This underwritten offering, which is fully guaranteed, is expected to settle around February 25, 2026, and will be used primarily to repay commercial paper borrowings associated with a previous cash tender offer for older senior notes. This strategic move aims to extend debt maturities and enhance the company's balance-sheet flexibility by reducing reliance on short-term funding.

feed.title: Braskem Denies Reported 2025 Default on Banco do Brasil Obligations
feed.summary: Braskem SA clarified on February 12, 2026, that media reports of a default on Banco do Brasil obligations in late 2025 were false. The company stated it had no material financial exposure to Banco do Brasil in 2025 and is current on all commitments, aiming to reassure the market about its financial health. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates BAK as Neutral, citing high leverage and declining profitability despite some positive aspects like improved EBITDA and liquidity.

feed.title: Live Nation Break Up Is Less Likely After DOJ Overhaul. What Would a Deal Look Like?
feed.summary: Following the departure of Assistant Attorney General Gail Slater, Live Nation might be closer to an out-of-court settlement with the Department of Justice regarding its monopoly suit. The potential deal is unlikely to involve a full breakup of Live Nation and Ticketmaster, but rather smaller concessions such as divestitures in artist management, an end to exclusivity agreements, or enhanced pricing transparency. This development comes despite calls from groups like NIVA for a complete dissolution of the Live Nation-Ticketmaster merger, with many states that joined the lawsuit still expected to pursue litigation if a settlement is reached.
